behavior_in_task_description.reason: All file paths (packages/react-router/src/Match.tsx, packages/solid-router/src/Match.tsx, packages/solid-router/src/not-found.tsx, packages/vue-router/src/Match.tsx), function names (getNotFound), and literal string patterns (error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId, notFoundError.routeId ??=, etc.) checked in tests are explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies exact implementation details: 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId' appearing twice, 'getNotFound' function name, '(error as any)?.cause' pattern, 'error.routeId ??= matchData.value?.routeId as any' - these are the exact code changes, not just a description of what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests like test_react_match_assigns_routeId_onCatch assert exact string 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId' via _file_contains. An alternative correct fix using e.g. 'error.routeId = error.routeId ?? matchState.routeId' or a different variable name would fail. test_solid_notFound_exports_getNotFound checks for exact string 'export function getNotFound' and '(error as any)?.cause'. test_vue_match_assigns_routeId checks for exact string 'error.routeId ??= matchData.value?.routeId as any'.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_react_match_assigns_routeId_onCatch, test_react_match_assigns_routeId_in_fallback, test_solid_match_uses_getNotFound_helper, test_solid_notFound_exports_getNotFound, test_vue_match_assigns_routeId) are ALL text-matching via _file_contains checking for literal strings in source files. While pass-to-pass tests run actual test suites, the core f2p tests are purely text-based.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only check if specific strings exist in files (e.g., test_react_match_assigns_routeId_onCatch checks 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId' in Match.tsx). An agent could simply append a comment containing that string to the file, or add dead code containing the pattern, without actually fixing the bug.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/router accessible to the agent. The agent could read solve.sh or the gold patch from /solution/ directory. Also, since f2p tests are pure string matching, the agent could grep test files to find the exact strings being checked and insert them as dead code.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in instruction.md (React routeId assignment in onCatch and fallback, Solid getNotFound helper with cause unwrapping, Vue routeId assignment) are tested, plus pass-to-pass tests verify existing test suites, types, and eslint pass.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped with comments ('Fail-to-pass tests' and 'Pass-to-pass tests'), each has a descriptive docstring explaining purpose, and naming follows clear conventions.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code fixes, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, commands, and variable names in instruction.md and tests appear correct and consistent.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names exact files, exact code patterns ('error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId'), exact function names to create ('getNotFound'), and exact locations within those files (onCatch handler, fallback handler). This goes far beyond describing what's broken.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config edit task.
